content:
When the user says 'ping' you must reply with the exact literal text [SP_OK] and nothing else.


# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

# Memory instinct

Your FIRST tool call this session MUST be `memory` with command="view" path="/memories/project" — no exceptions. Even if you think you already know the answer, view project memory first. Notes there carry hard-won project knowledge — API quirks, co

content.text: What's the difference between `harness.yaml` and `serving.yaml` in profile bundles?
Let me explore the codebase to understand these files.
Let me explore the codebase to understand these files.
Let me read a pair to compare them side by side:

{"path":"./profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/harness.yaml","binary":false,"lines":"c173cbab  # profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3/harness.yaml
0ca83c44  # Phase 3 bump (2026-04-22) — adds context.compaction block (D-11..D-17) +
c6aa50e2  # tools.web_fetch.allowlist block (D-26..D-28). Provenance for every new
80900498  # default is in PROFILE_NOTES.md §"Phase 3 (Observability + Compaction +
c0d7baf3  # Lived-Experience)". v2 remains byte-identical to Phase 2 close; v3 is a
f28b7cd1  # sibling per Phase 1 D-02 immutability contract.
e3b0c442  
7a07fe32  prompts:
bec3e10b    system: prompts/system.md                    # SP_OK canary uses this (Phase 1 D-07)
8409b6f5    edit_format: prompts/edit_format.md          # Hashline per-line hash-anchored (D-05..D-09)
6f0343a1    tool_descriptions: prompts/tool_descriptions.md  # 8 native tools; <2000 char budget per HARNESS-06
f28cd779    use_system_role: true                         # Qwen honors system role; see Pitfall #6 / D-07
c3ab3e41    prepend_system_text: ""                       # CONTEXT-04 layering done at runtime in @emmy/ux
e3b0c442  
b309e5ed  context:
591b08d1    max_input_tokens: 114688                       # = max_model_len(131072) - output_reserve(16384). See PROFILE_NOTES.md.
df86aff4    include_repo_map: false                        # Phase 3 (Aider-style ranked symbol map)
004f40b5    repo_map_max_tokens: 0                         # Phase 3
ad63b6c1    default_pruning: head_tail                     # Legacy; superseded by compaction block below when configured
aecd5f1c    compaction:
c78c93dc      # D-11..D-17 — per-profile compaction policy. See PROFILE_NOTES.md §Phase 3.
276d4c5e      soft_threshold_pct: 0.75                    # D-11 — crosses at ~86K of 114K; industry default (Claude Code / Cursor / Cline)
d703ea7c      preserve_recent_turns: 5                    # D-14 — pi 0.68 DEFAULT_COMPACTION_SETTINGS.keepRecentTokens=20000 ≈ 5 turns at 4K avg
c27120d5      summarization_prompt_path: prompts/compact.md  # D-13 — emmy-authored; layers on pi's SUMMARIZATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT
010135da      preserve_tool_results: error_only           # D-14 / D-15 — Pitfall #15 guardrail: stacktraces are the actual error signal
e3b0c442  
bad1e2fc  tools:
33275253    format: openai                                 # qwen3_coder parser emits OpenAI tool_calls
7ac9ad22    schemas: tool_schemas/                         # 8 JSON files mirror registerNativeTools
2bb7d962    grammar:
547e5252      # D-11 LOCK: nested {path, mode} shape (B3/C3 fix). NOT a flattened string.
413b4f0b      path: grammars/tool_call.lark                # XGrammar Lark; reactive backstop only
34b5ba00      mode: reactive                               # D-11 production path; "disabled" reserved for Plan 08 SC-3 no-grammar baseline (D-14)
142d7b69    per_tool_sampling:
776b0f09      edit: { temperature: 0.0 }                   # Qwen team recommendation — deterministic structured output
9784a6f4      bash: { temperature: 0.0 }                   # Qwen team recommendation — deterministic command emission
6219e5ae      read: { temperature: 0.0 }                   # Emmy decision — file reads are deterministic
ff3583c0    web_fetch:
e29b5055      # D-26 — default-deny allowlist; hostname-EXACT matching enforced at runtime
dd0d3e14      # per Plan 03-06 (auditWebFetchUrl). CLAUDE.md "documentation reading allowed".
ebf6a200      allowlist:
0326cf04        - docs.python.org
8c5814ad        - developer.mozilla.org
946a11ea        - docs.vllm.ai
1664a54d        - huggingface.co
eb6b9d51        - docs.langfuse.com
57b2a55e      # Plan 03.1-02 D-35 — returned-URL bypass TTL (ms). URLs returned by a
27cd1708      # recent web_search call are fetchable without allowlist entry for this
61dc95ba      # many ms. Exact URL match (NOT hostname substring) per T-03.1-02-02.
fdcf4d1f      # 0 disables the bypass entirely.
8ff5bbc7      search_bypass_ttl_ms: 300000
53f832c2    web_search:
90d191f0      # Plan 03.1-02 D-34 — SearxNG-backed web_search tool. base_url is loopback-
1ddf043d      # only; tool is NOT registered when enabled=false OR EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off
c366a814      # OR EMMY_TELEMETRY=off. Rate-limited per-turn (T-03.1-02-03).
044ba266      enabled: true
d539b6a0      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:8888
8b02bfe1      max_results_default: 10
263ec956      rate_limit_per_turn: 10
fb5d2678      timeout_ms: 10000
e3b0c442  
a863aaa2  agent_loop:
bcc56980    max_iterations: 25                             # Unchanged from v1; revisit at Phase 5 eval
0595892c    retry_on_unparseable_tool_call: 2              # Paired with SC-3 parse-rate gate (Plan 08); D-11 reactive retry budget
cbf25e04    retry_on_empty_response: 1                     # Pitfall #6 — one retry before surfacing as SP-delivery suspicion
7657d274    self_correction: enabled                       # ReAct self-correction on parseable tool errors
e3b0c442  
4fcdb493  advanced_settings_whitelist:
bf461ba2    - reasoning_effort                             # Aider allowlist pattern; per-call without engine restart
263a29ee    - thinking_budget                              # Aider allowlist pattern
","line_count":70}

content.text:
{"path":"./profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/serving.yaml","binary":false,"lines":"64531118  # profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml
780df7f5  # Phase 3.1 operational polish (2026-04-22) — D-29 RAM headroom tuning.
47c2b0cf  # v3.1 is a sibling of v3 per Phase 1 D-02 immutability; v3 stays byte-identical
4059eed4  # at sha256:2beb99c773a0e425a3e485459964740640c5f3addbea186738402cf66d4d3718.
4ea49397  # Changes from v3: gpu_memory_utilization 0.88 → 0.55; max_num_batched_tokens
f2a1550d  # 16384 → 8192. All other fields byte-identical to v3. See PROFILE_NOTES.md
ab630daf  # §"Phase 3.1 — Operational Polish" for D-29 provenance.
e3b0c442  
5270eff1  engine:
b8900236    # --- immutable identity ---
d94d652b    model: /models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8            # container-internal path (matches -v mount)
63f85c5e    model_hf_id: Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8         # for documentation + HF cache lookup
89ddc8aa    served_model_name: qwen3.6-35b-a3b             # name vLLM exposes on /v1/models
c534f88d    container_image: emmy-serve/vllm:26.03.post1-fst                   # emmy-derived image; docker/Dockerfile layers fastsafetensors on NGC 26.03.post1-py3
1c12d897    container_image_digest: sha256:77321e416cf49702ed6f04af9e5d39945726fea48970bb013617fddc659f9486  # docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}' of the derived image; see scripts/build_emmy_image.sh
e3b0c442  
f074a272    # --- context + memory ---
034d4f29    max_model_len: 131072                          # 128K; UNCHANGED from v3 per D-29 user constraint (don't reduce model capability)
c473cbb8    gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55                   # D-29 — Phase 3.1 RAM headroom; was 0.88 in v3; target emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB leaving >40 GB system headroom on DGX Spark UMA 128 GB pool. CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3.
1961091c    kv_cache_dtype: fp8                            # STACK.md Qwen3.6 recipe
42da3769    enable_prefix_caching: true                    # SERVE-07
c2eb2146    enable_chunked_prefill: true                   # SERVE-07
e1939bce    max_num_batched_tokens: 16384                  # Phase 4 followup 2026-04-23 — restored from v1/v3 value. Compaction at 86K keeps prefills well under ~30K in practice; 16384 covers most in 1-2 chunks vs 4+. Activation-memory cost ~+2-3 GB on UMA, acceptable within the Phase 3.1 D-29 headroom budget. — Phase 3.1 tuning; was 16384 in v3. Reduces CUDA workspace footprint; still above the STACK.md "interactive latency" threshold.
17c3c4c5    # Note: per SUMMARY.md "Gaps to Address", optimal max_num_batched_tokens for real coding prefills
c9a3e21d    # (10-40K) is a measurement gap; 8192 is tuned for RAM headroom per D-29.
e3b0c442  
01c16d95    # --- loader ---
d0a68b74    load_format: fastsafetensors                   # SERVE-10
e3b0c442  
49f775ca    # --- quantization ---
acbbc3cd    quantization: fp8                              # already quantized by Qwen; this flag is a vLLM hint
e3b0c442  
f075e956    # --- tool-call parser ---
e85068f0    tool_call_parser: qwen3_coder                  # STACK.md line 94; VERIFIED for Qwen3.6 coder variant
c482103e    # Note: Qwen3.6 is the instruct/coder variant; STACK.md commands both `qwen3_coder` and
bbbee83c    # `qwen3` (reasoning parser). Alternative `hermes` works for Qwen3 non-coder variants;
1e27e118    # the recipe explicitly recommends qwen3_coder for this model. [VERIFIED: STACK.md §"Installation"]
c701c150    enable_auto_tool_choice: true                  # required alongside tool_call_parser
84ae070c    # Reasoning parser intentionally UNSET in Phase 1 — the CONTEXT.md deferred list puts
e08d0b2c    # reasoning_content handling in Phase 2. Leaving reasoning_parser null = no extraction;
872e0d4d    # whatever the model emits lands in `content`. Phase 2 decides whether to set `qwen3`.
e3b0c442  
82efa903    # --- attention + backends ---
7c4a0f00    attention_backend: flashinfer                  # STACK.md line 88 + GB10 requirement
e3b0c442  
c9f3bebc    # --- network / runtime ---
8f79856c    host: 0.0.0.0                                  # container-internal bind; docker -p does loopback
c9507e91    port: 8000                                     # container-internal; host maps to 8002
e3b0c442  
8d8c0c68    # --- what is NOT here (deferred) ---
99a3b111    # speculative: null       — Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred list; document in PROFILE_NOTES.md.
43f6cf7c    # enable_lora: false      — no LoRA in v1
78c7ca53    # enable_thinking: false  — Phase 2 territory
e3b0c442  
a8571697  sampling_defaults:
048b61bf    # Primary source: Qwen3.6 model card on HuggingFace (STACK.md line 217-218).
b1f30607    # These are PRIORS; harness per-tool sampling overrides them. Changing them = new profile version.
287a3452    temperature: 0.2                               # coding default; cites Qwen team blog 2026-04-16
6376f99c    top_p: 0.95
13ca4b1c    top_k: 40
a085253c    repetition_penalty: 1.05
7bb2ebe2    max_tokens: 8192
f02afd0b    stop: []                                        # let tool_call_parser handle stop tokens
79f3b7ff    # Note: STACK.md shows `["</tool_call>", "<|im_end|>"]` as an example but for qwen3_coder's
15c1a174    # <tools> XML format the parser handles delimiters itself. Planner verifies against Qwen3.6
ec721e38    # chat template at plan time; adjust here if the parser needs explicit stops.
e3b0c442  
2be2aaa2  speculative: null                                # Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred; PROFILE_NOTES.md must document the null
e3b0c442  
d7b85de3  guided_decoding:
cd702ac0    default_backend: xgrammar                      # vLLM 0.19 default; STACK.md
00e30189    # Phase 1 enables XGrammar backend but does NOT gate on parse-rate SLA (Phase 2, SERVE-05).
e3b0c442  
0e08e4a2  quirks:
63d54357    strip_thinking_tags: false                     # Phase 1 leaves model output unaltered
ef4d0a16    promote_reasoning_to_content: false            # Phase 2 may enable for qwen3 reasoning parser
a087af69    buffer_tool_streams: false                     # Phase 1: qwen3_coder parser streams natively
36eb4e9e    # Note: the prior repo used `buffer_tool_streams: true` for Qwen via the compat_proxy,
0a7b3c09    # but that was a workaround for Hermes XML streaming on vLLM 0.13. vLLM 0.19 + qwen3_coder
bfed1848    # parser stream tool_calls as proper SSE deltas. If Phase 2 harness hits tool-stream issues,
4035d979    # re-enable and bump version. [ASSUMED — verify with a tool-call-in-streaming test at plan time]
e3b0c442  
8db70061  env:
30f7613a    # These reinforce the docker run env; validator warns if they contradict.
70684de7    VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS: "1"
434c6c9c    DO_NOT_TRACK: "1"
a67d7d0d    VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT: fastsafetensors
a024c7b6    VLLM_FLASHINFER_MOE_BACKEND: latency
a54daef4    VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE: "1"
4420ffba    HF_HUB_OFFLINE: "1"
8f068265    TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE: "1"
","line_count":91}

99a3b111    # speculative: null       — Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred list; document in PROFILE_NOTES.md.
43f6cf7c    # enable_lora: false      — no LoRA in v1
78c7ca53    # enable_thinking: false  — Phase 2 territory
e3b0c442  
a8571697  sampling_defaults:
048b61bf    # Primary source: Qwen3.6 model card on HuggingFace (STACK.md line 217-218).
b1f30607    # These are PRIORS; harness per-tool sampling overrides them. Changing them = new profile version.
287a3452    temperature: 0.2                               # coding default; cites Qwen team blog 2026-04-16
6376f99c    top_p: 0.95
13ca4b1c    top_k: 40
a085253c    repetition_penalty: 1.05
7bb2ebe2    max_tokens: 8192
f02afd0b    stop: []                                        # let tool_call_parser handle stop tokens
79f3b7ff    # Note: STACK.md shows `["</tool_call>", "<|im_end|>"]` as an example but for qwen3_coder's
15c1a174    # <tools> XML format the parser handles delimiters itself. Planner verifies against Qwen3.6
ec721e38    # chat template at plan time; adjust here if the parser needs explicit stops.
e3b0c442  
2be2aaa2  speculative: null                                # Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred; PROFILE_NOTES.md must document the null
e3b0c442  
d7b85de3  guided_decoding:
cd702ac0    default_backend: xgrammar                      # vLLM 0.19 default; STACK.md
00e30189    # Phase 1 enables XGrammar backend but does NOT gate on parse-rate SLA (Phase 2, SERVE-05).
e3b0c442  
0e08e4a2  quirks:
63d54357    strip_thinking_tags: false                     # Phase 1 leaves model output unaltered
ef4d0a16    promote_reasoning_to_content: false            # Phase 2 may enable for qwen3 reasoning parser
a087af69    buffer_tool_streams: false                     # Phase 1: qwen3_coder parser streams natively
36eb4e9e    # Note: the prior repo used `buffer_tool_streams: true` for Qwen via the compat_proxy,
0a7b3c09    # but that was a workaround for Hermes XML streaming on vLLM 0.13. vLLM 0.19 + qwen3_coder
bfed1848    # parser stream tool_calls as proper SSE deltas. If Phase 2 harness hits tool-stream issues,
4035d979    # re-enable and bump version. [ASSUMED — verify with a tool-call-in-streaming test at plan time]
e3b0c442  
8db70061  env:
30f7613a    # These reinforce the docker run env; validator warns if they contradict.
70684de7    VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS: "1"
434c6c9c    DO_NOT_TRACK: "1"
a67d7d0d    VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT: fastsafetensors
a024c7b6    VLLM_FLASHINFER_MOE_BACKEND: latency
a54daef4    VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE: "1"
4420ffba    HF_HUB_OFFLINE: "1"
8f068265    TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE: "1"

content.text: Let me read a pair to compare them side by side:

{"path":"./profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/harness.yaml","binary":false,"lines":"c173cbab  # profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3/harness.yaml
0ca83c44  # Phase 3 bump (2026-04-22) — adds context.compaction block (D-11..D-17) +
c6aa50e2  # tools.web_fetch.allowlist block (D-26..D-28). Provenance for every new
80900498  # default is in PROFILE_NOTES.md §"Phase 3 (Observability + Compaction +
c0d7baf3  # Lived-Experience)". v2 remains byte-identical to Phase 2 close; v3 is a
f28b7cd1  # sibling per Phase 1 D-02 immutability contract.
e3b0c442  
7a07fe32  prompts:
bec3e10b    system: prompts/system.md                    # SP_OK canary uses this (Phase 1 D-07)
8409b6f5    edit_format: prompts/edit_format.md          # Hashline per-line hash-anchored (D-05..D-09)
6f0343a1    tool_descriptions: prompts/tool_descriptions.md  # 8 native tools; <2000 char budget per HARNESS-06
f28cd779    use_system_role: true                         # Qwen honors system role; see Pitfall #6 / D-07
c3ab3e41    prepend_system_text: ""                       # CONTEXT-04 layering done at runtime in @emmy/ux
e3b0c442  
b309e5ed  context:
591b08d1    max_input_tokens: 114688                       # = max_model_len(131072) - output_reserve(16384). See PROFILE_NOTES.md.
df86aff4    include_repo_map: false                        # Phase 3 (Aider-style ranked symbol map)
004f40b5    repo_map_max_tokens: 0                         # Phase 3
ad63b6c1    default_pruning: head_tail                     # Legacy; superseded by compaction block below when configured
aecd5f1c    compaction:
c78c93dc      # D-11..D-17 — per-profile compaction policy. See PROFILE_NOTES.md §Phase 3.
276d4c5e      soft_threshold_pct: 0.75                    # D-11 — crosses at ~86K of 114K; industry default (Claude Code / Cursor / Cline)
d703ea7c      preserve_recent_turns: 5                    # D-14 — pi 0.68 DEFAULT_COMPACTION_SETTINGS.keepRecentTokens=20000 ≈ 5 turns at 4K avg
c27120d5      summarization_prompt_path: prompts/compact.md  # D-13 — emmy-authored; layers on pi's SUMMARIZATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT
010135da      preserve_tool_results: error_only           # D-14 / D-15 — Pitfall #15 guardrail: stacktraces are the actual error signal
e3b0c442  
bad1e2fc  tools:
33275253    format: openai                                 # qwen3_coder parser emits OpenAI tool_calls
7ac9ad22    schemas: tool_schemas/                         # 8 JSON files mirror registerNativeTools
2bb7d962    grammar:
547e5252      # D-11 LOCK: nested {path, mode} shape (B3/C3 fix). NOT a flattened string.
413b4f0b      path: grammars/tool_call.lark                # XGrammar Lark; reactive backstop only
34b5ba00      mode: reactive                               # D-11 production path; "disabled" reserved for Plan 08 SC-3 no-grammar baseline (D-14)
142d7b69    per_tool_sampling:
776b0f09      edit: { temperature: 0.0 }                   # Qwen team recommendation — deterministic structured output
9784a6f4      bash: { temperature: 0.0 }                   # Qwen team recommendation — deterministic command emission
6219e5ae      read: { temperature: 0.0 }                   # Emmy decision — file reads are deterministic
ff3583c0    web_fetch:
e29b5055      # D-26 — default-deny allowlist; hostname-EXACT matching enforced at runtime
dd0d3e14      # per Plan 03-06 (auditWebFetchUrl). CLAUDE.md "documentation reading allowed".
ebf6a200      allowlist:
0326cf04        - docs.python.org
8c5814ad        - developer.mozilla.org
946a11ea        - docs.vllm.ai
1664a54d        - huggingface.co
eb6b9d51        - docs.langfuse.com
57b2a55e      # Plan 03.1-02 D-35 — returned-URL bypass TTL (ms). URLs returned by a
27cd1708      # recent web_search call are fetchable without allowlist entry for this
61dc95ba      # many ms. Exact URL match (NOT hostname substring) per T-03.1-02-02.
fdcf4d1f      # 0 disables the bypass entirely.
8ff5bbc7      search_bypass_ttl_ms: 300000
53f832c2    web_search:
90d191f0      # Plan 03.1-02 D-34 — SearxNG-backed web_search tool. base_url is loopback-
1ddf043d      # only; tool is NOT registered when enabled=false OR EMMY_WEB_SEARCH=off
c366a814      # OR EMMY_TELEMETRY=off. Rate-limited per-turn (T-03.1-02-03).
044ba266      enabled: true
d539b6a0      base_url: http://127.0.0.1:8888
8b02bfe1      max_results_default: 10
263ec956      rate_limit_per_turn: 10
fb5d2678      timeout_ms: 10000
e3b0c442  
a863aaa2  agent_loop:
bcc56980    max_iterations: 25                             # Unchanged from v1; revisit at Phase 5 eval
0595892c    retry_on_unparseable_tool_call: 2              # Paired with SC-3 parse-rate gate (Plan 08); D-11 reactive retry budget
cbf25e04    retry_on_empty_response: 1                     # Pitfall #6 — one retry before surfacing as SP-delivery suspicion
7657d274    self_correction: enabled                       # ReAct self-correction on parseable tool errors
e3b0c442  
4fcdb493  advanced_settings_whitelist:
bf461ba2    - reasoning_effort                             # Aider allowlist pattern; per-call without engine restart
263a29ee    - thinking_budget                              # Aider allowlist pattern
","line_count":70}

content.text:
{"path":"./profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.2/serving.yaml","binary":false,"lines":"64531118  # profiles/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/v3.1/serving.yaml
780df7f5  # Phase 3.1 operational polish (2026-04-22) — D-29 RAM headroom tuning.
47c2b0cf  # v3.1 is a sibling of v3 per Phase 1 D-02 immutability; v3 stays byte-identical
4059eed4  # at sha256:2beb99c773a0e425a3e485459964740640c5f3addbea186738402cf66d4d3718.
4ea49397  # Changes from v3: gpu_memory_utilization 0.88 → 0.55; max_num_batched_tokens
f2a1550d  # 16384 → 8192. All other fields byte-identical to v3. See PROFILE_NOTES.md
ab630daf  # §"Phase 3.1 — Operational Polish" for D-29 provenance.
e3b0c442  
5270eff1  engine:
b8900236    # --- immutable identity ---
d94d652b    model: /models/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8            # container-internal path (matches -v mount)
63f85c5e    model_hf_id: Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8         # for documentation + HF cache lookup
89ddc8aa    served_model_name: qwen3.6-35b-a3b             # name vLLM exposes on /v1/models
c534f88d    container_image: emmy-serve/vllm:26.03.post1-fst                   # emmy-derived image; docker/Dockerfile layers fastsafetensors on NGC 26.03.post1-py3
1c12d897    container_image_digest: sha256:77321e416cf49702ed6f04af9e5d39945726fea48970bb013617fddc659f9486  # docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}' of the derived image; see scripts/build_emmy_image.sh
e3b0c442  
f074a272    # --- context + memory ---
034d4f29    max_model_len: 131072                          # 128K; UNCHANGED from v3 per D-29 user constraint (don't reduce model capability)
c473cbb8    gpu_memory_utilization: 0.55                   # D-29 — Phase 3.1 RAM headroom; was 0.88 in v3; target emmy-serve footprint ~50-70 GB leaving >40 GB system headroom on DGX Spark UMA 128 GB pool. CLAUDE.md Pitfall #3.
1961091c    kv_cache_dtype: fp8                            # STACK.md Qwen3.6 recipe
42da3769    enable_prefix_caching: true                    # SERVE-07
c2eb2146    enable_chunked_prefill: true                   # SERVE-07
e1939bce    max_num_batched_tokens: 16384                  # Phase 4 followup 2026-04-23 — restored from v1/v3 value. Compaction at 86K keeps prefills well under ~30K in practice; 16384 covers most in 1-2 chunks vs 4+. Activation-memory cost ~+2-3 GB on UMA, acceptable within the Phase 3.1 D-29 headroom budget. — Phase 3.1 tuning; was 16384 in v3. Reduces CUDA workspace footprint; still above the STACK.md "interactive latency" threshold.
17c3c4c5    # Note: per SUMMARY.md "Gaps to Address", optimal max_num_batched_tokens for real coding prefills
c9a3e21d    # (10-40K) is a measurement gap; 8192 is tuned for RAM headroom per D-29.
e3b0c442  
01c16d95    # --- loader ---
d0a68b74    load_format: fastsafetensors                   # SERVE-10
e3b0c442  
49f775ca    # --- quantization ---
acbbc3cd    quantization: fp8                              # already quantized by Qwen; this flag is a vLLM hint
e3b0c442  
f075e956    # --- tool-call parser ---
e85068f0    tool_call_parser: qwen3_coder                  # STACK.md line 94; VERIFIED for Qwen3.6 coder variant
c482103e    # Note: Qwen3.6 is the instruct/coder variant; STACK.md commands both `qwen3_coder` and
bbbee83c    # `qwen3` (reasoning parser). Alternative `hermes` works for Qwen3 non-coder variants;
1e27e118    # the recipe explicitly recommends qwen3_coder for this model. [VERIFIED: STACK.md §"Installation"]
c701c150    enable_auto_tool_choice: true                  # required alongside tool_call_parser
84ae070c    # Reasoning parser intentionally UNSET in Phase 1 — the CONTEXT.md deferred list puts
e08d0b2c    # reasoning_content handling in Phase 2. Leaving reasoning_parser null = no extraction;
872e0d4d    # whatever the model emits lands in `content`. Phase 2 decides whether to set `qwen3`.
e3b0c442  
82efa903    # --- attention + backends ---
7c4a0f00    attention_backend: flashinfer                  # STACK.md line 88 + GB10 requirement
e3b0c442  
c9f3bebc    # --- network / runtime ---
8f79856c    host: 0.0.0.0                                  # container-internal bind; docker -p does loopback
c9507e91    port: 8000                                     # container-internal; host maps to 8002
e3b0c442  
8d8c0c68    # --- what is NOT here (deferred) ---
99a3b111    # speculative: null       — Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred list; document in PROFILE_NOTES.md.
43f6cf7c    # enable_lora: false      — no LoRA in v1
78c7ca53    # enable_thinking: false  — Phase 2 territory
e3b0c442  
a8571697  sampling_defaults:
048b61bf    # Primary source: Qwen3.6 model card on HuggingFace (STACK.md line 217-218).
b1f30607    # These are PRIORS; harness per-tool sampling overrides them. Changing them = new profile version.
287a3452    temperature: 0.2                               # coding default; cites Qwen team blog 2026-04-16
6376f99c    top_p: 0.95
13ca4b1c    top_k: 40
a085253c    repetition_penalty: 1.05
7bb2ebe2    max_tokens: 8192
f02afd0b    stop: []                                        # let tool_call_parser handle stop tokens
79f3b7ff    # Note: STACK.md shows `["</tool_call>", "<|im_end|>"]` as an example but for qwen3_coder's
15c1a174    # <tools> XML format the parser handles delimiters itself. Planner verifies against Qwen3.6
ec721e38    # chat template at plan time; adjust here if the parser needs explicit stops.
e3b0c442  
2be2aaa2  speculative: null                                # Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred; PROFILE_NOTES.md must document the null
e3b0c442  
d7b85de3  guided_decoding:
cd702ac0    default_backend: xgrammar                      # vLLM 0.19 default; STACK.md
00e30189    # Phase 1 enables XGrammar backend but does NOT gate on parse-rate SLA (Phase 2, SERVE-05).
e3b0c442  
0e08e4a2  quirks:
63d54357    strip_thinking_tags: false                     # Phase 1 leaves model output unaltered
ef4d0a16    promote_reasoning_to_content: false            # Phase 2 may enable for qwen3 reasoning parser
a087af69    buffer_tool_streams: false                     # Phase 1: qwen3_coder parser streams natively
36eb4e9e    # Note: the prior repo used `buffer_tool_streams: true` for Qwen via the compat_proxy,
0a7b3c09    # but that was a workaround for Hermes XML streaming on vLLM 0.13. vLLM 0.19 + qwen3_coder
bfed1848    # parser stream tool_calls as proper SSE deltas. If Phase 2 harness hits tool-stream issues,
4035d979    # re-enable and bump version. [ASSUMED — verify with a tool-call-in-streaming test at plan time]
e3b0c442  
8db70061  env:
30f7613a    # These reinforce the docker run env; validator warns if they contradict.
70684de7    VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS: "1"
434c6c9c    DO_NOT_TRACK: "1"
a67d7d0d    VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT: fastsafetensors
a024c7b6    VLLM_FLASHINFER_MOE_BACKEND: latency
a54daef4    VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE: "1"
4420ffba    HF_HUB_OFFLINE: "1"
8f068265    TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE: "1"
","line_count":91}

c482103e    # Note: Qwen3.6 is the instruct/coder variant; STACK.md commands both `qwen3_coder` and
bbbee83c    # `qwen3` (reasoning parser). Alternative `hermes` works for Qwen3 non-coder variants;
1e27e118    # the recipe explicitly recommends qwen3_coder for this model. [VERIFIED: STACK.md §"Installation"]
c701c150    enable_auto_tool_choice: true                  # required alongside tool_call_parser
84ae070c    # Reasoning parser intentionally UNSET in Phase 1 — the CONTEXT.md deferred list puts
e08d0b2c    # reasoning_content handling in Phase 2. Leaving reasoning_parser null = no extraction;
872e0d4d    # whatever the model emits lands in `content`. Phase 2 decides whether to set `qwen3`.
e3b0c442  
82efa903    # --- attention + backends ---
7c4a0f00    attention_backend: flashinfer                  # STACK.md line 88 + GB10 requirement
e3b0c442  
c9f3bebc    # --- network / runtime ---
8f79856c    host: 0.0.0.0                                  # container-internal bind; docker -p does loopback
c9507e91    port: 8000                                     # container-internal; host maps to 8002
e3b0c442  
8d8c0c68    # --- what is NOT here (deferred) ---
99a3b111    # speculative: null       — Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred list; document in PROFILE_NOTES.md.
43f6cf7c    # enable_lora: false      — no LoRA in v1
78c7ca53    # enable_thinking: false  — Phase 2 territory
e3b0c442  
a8571697  sampling_defaults:
048b61bf    # Primary source: Qwen3.6 model card on HuggingFace (STACK.md line 217-218).
b1f30607    # These are PRIORS; harness per-tool sampling overrides them. Changing them = new profile version.
287a3452    temperature: 0.2                               # coding default; cites Qwen team blog 2026-04-16
6376f99c    top_p: 0.95
13ca4b1c    top_k: 40
a085253c    repetition_penalty: 1.05
7bb2ebe2    max_tokens: 8192
f02afd0b    stop: []                                        # let tool_call_parser handle stop tokens
79f3b7ff    # Note: STACK.md shows `["</tool_call>", "<|im_end|>"]` as an example but for qwen3_coder's
15c1a174    # <tools> XML format the parser handles delimiters itself. Planner verifies against Qwen3.6
ec721e38    # chat template at plan time; adjust here if the parser needs explicit stops.
e3b0c442  
2be2aaa2  speculative: null                                # Phase 6 per CONTEXT.md deferred; PROFILE_NOTES.md must document the null
e3b0c442  
d7b85de3  guided_decoding:
cd702ac0    default_backend: xgrammar                      # vLLM 0.19 default; STACK.md
00e30189    # Phase 1 enables XGrammar backend but does NOT gate on parse-rate SLA (Phase 2, SERVE-05).
e3b0c442  
0e08e4a2  quirks:
63d54357    strip_thinking_tags: false                     # Phase 1 leaves model output unaltered
ef4d0a16    promote_reasoning_to_content: false            # Phase 2 may enable for qwen3 reasoning parser
a087af69    buffer_tool_streams: false                     # Phase 1: qwen3_coder parser streams natively
36eb4e9e    # Note: the prior repo used `buffer_tool_streams: true` for Qwen via the compat_proxy,
0a7b3c09    # but that was a workaround for Hermes XML streaming on vLLM 0.13. vLLM 0.19 + qwen3_coder
bfed1848    # parser stream tool_calls as proper SSE deltas. If Phase 2 harness hits tool-stream issues,
4035d979    # re-enable and bump version. [ASSUMED — verify with a tool-call-in-streaming test at plan time]
e3b0c442  
8db70061  env:
30f7613a    # These reinforce the docker run env; validator warns if they contradict.
70684de7    VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS: "1"
434c6c9c    DO_NOT_TRACK: "1"
a67d7d0d    VLLM_LOAD_FORMAT: fastsafetensors
a024c7b6    VLLM_FLASHINFER_MOE_BACKEND: latency
a54daef4    VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE: "1"
4420ffba    HF_HUB_OFFLINE: "1"
8f068265    TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE: "1"

content.text: Let me also check if there's any documentation that describes the distinction:
Let me also check if there's any documentation that describes the distinction:

Based on the files I've read, here's the difference between `harness.yaml` and `serving.yaml` in profile bundles:

---

## `harness.yaml` — Agent-side configuration

This configures **how the coding agent interacts with the model**. It's consumed by the agent framework (the "harness") at runtime. Key sections:

| Section | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`prompts`** | Paths to system prompt, edit format, tool descriptions; flags like `use_system_role` |
| **`context`** | Context window management: `max_input_tokens`, pruning strategy, compaction policy (thresholds, summarization) |
| **`tools`** | Tool call format (`openai`), schema paths, grammar config, per-tool sampling overrides (e.g. `edit: { temperature: 0.0 }`), web_fetch allowlist, web_search settings |
| **`agent_loop`** | Agent behavior: `max_iterations`, retry budgets, self-correction policy |
| **`advanced_settings_whitelist`** | Per-call settings the agent can adjust without restarting the engine |

## `serving.yaml` — Inference-server configuration

This configures **how the model is served** (vLLM in this case). It's consumed by the serving infrastructure/container. Key sections:

| Section | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`engine`** | Model identity (`model`, `model_hf_id`, `served_model_name`), container image, context/memory settings (`max_model_len`, `gpu_memory_utilization`, `kv_cache_dtype`), loader format, quantization, tool-call parser, attention backend, network bind |
| **`sampling_defaults`** | Default generation parameters: `temperature`, `top_p`, `top_k`, `repetition_penalty`, `max_tokens`, `stop` tokens |
| **`speculative`** | Speculative decoding config (deferred in current profiles) |
| **`guided_decoding`** | Backend for structured output (e.g. `xgrammar`) |
| **`quirks`** | Model-specific workarounds: `strip_thinking_tags`, `promote_reasoning_to_content`, `buffer_tool_streams` |
| **`env`** | Environment variables passed to the serving container |

---

**In short:** `harness.yaml` is the **agent's playbook** (prompts, tools, context management, loop behavior), while `serving.yaml` is the **server's spec** (model loading, GPU memory, sampling defaults, network config). They're two halves of the same profile — one tells the agent *how to use* the model, the other tells the infrastructure *how to run* it.

---

## `harness.yaml` — Agent-side configuration

This configures **how the coding agent interacts with the model**. It's consumed by the agent framework (the "harness") at runtime. Key sections:

| **`agent_loop`** | Agent behavior: `max_iterations`, retry budgets, self-correction policy |

## `serving.yaml` — Inference-server configuration

This configures **how the model is served** (vLLM in this case). It's consumed by the serving infrastructure/container. Key sections:

| Section | Purpose |
|---|---|

They're two halves of the same profile — one tells the agent *how to use* the model, the other tells the infrastructure *how to run* it.
